title: spanda
text: This file will become your README and also the index of your documentation.

title: spanda
text:
How to use
Fill me in please! Don’t forget code examples:

1+1

2

title: Training a XResnet
text:
from spanda.data.transforms.kssl import (get_spectra_files, to_spectra, rand_w_avg, AnalytesTfm)

from pathlib import Path
from fastai.vision.all import *
from fastai.data.all import *


path = Path('../../_data/kssl-mirs')


# Selecting only Kex > 0 values
paths = L(filter(lambda x: AnalytesTfm(analytes=[725])(x).item() > 0, path.ls()))


x_tfms = [get_spectra_files, to_spectra, rand_w_avg]
y_tfms = [AnalytesTfm(analytes=[725]), torch.log10]


splits = RandomSplitter(seed=42)(paths)
dsets = Datasets(paths, [x_tfms, y_tfms], splits=splits)
dls = dsets.dataloaders(bs=16)
dls.train.one_batch()[0].shape

torch.Size([16, 1, 1700])



torch.cuda.is_available()

False



dls.train.one_batch()[0].dtype

torch.float32



x.dtype

torch.float32



model = xresnet18(ndim=1, c_in=1, ks=15, n_out=1)
x = dls.train.one_batch()[0]
model(x)

TensorBase([[0.4673],
            [0.3595],
            [0.3177],
            [0.3937],
            [0.5398],
            [0.4144],
            [0.2420],
            [0.3702],
            [0.3172],
            [0.1935],
            [0.3136],
            [0.2349],
            [0.1923],
            [0.3886],
            [0.4451],
            [0.2703]], grad_fn=<AliasBackward0>)



#learn = Learner(dls, xresnet18, loss_func=MSELossFlat(), y_range=(-1,1))
learn = Learner(dls, model, loss_func=MSELossFlat())


learn.fit_one_cycle(1)







    
      
      0.00% [0/1 00:00<?]
    
    

  
    
      epoch
      train_loss
      valid_loss
      time

title: KSSL
text:
How to use these transforms?

First create two // pipes (one for the features and one for the targets):


x_tfms = [get_spectra_files, to_spectra, rand_w_avg]
y_tfms = [AnalytesTfm(analytes=[725]), torch.log10]


Create your splits and create a Fastai Datasets:


splits = RandomSplitter(seed=42)(path.ls())
dsets = Datasets(path.ls(), [x_tfms, y_tfms], splits=splits)


Then you get your Dataloader:


dls = dsets.dataloaders(bs=16)


dls.train.one_batch()[0].shape

torch.Size([16, 4, 1700])



dls.train.one_batch()[1]

Analyte([[-4.8662e-01],
         [-4.7290e-01],
         [ 4.8377e-01],
         [-9.2032e-01],
         [-6.1871e-01],
         [ 2.9790e-04],
         [-1.1769e-01],
         [-5.0147e-01],
         [-4.6117e-01],
         [-2.0003e-01],
         [       -inf],
         [-9.4657e-01],
         [-3.8762e-01],
         [-1.2586e-01],
         [-1.3596e+00],
         [-9.3043e-01]])



dls.show_batch(max_n=4)

title: Core
text:
source

interp

 interp (x:Tuple[numpy.ndarray,numpy.ndarray], between:Tuple=(4000, 600),
         res:int=2, kind:str='linear')

Interpolate spectra within the proper range and at resolution of interest










Type
Default
Details




x
typing.Tuple[numpy.ndarray, numpy.ndarray]

(spectrum, wavenumbers)


between
typing.Tuple
(4000, 600)
wavenumbers range of interest


res
int
2
wavenumbers resolution


kind
str
linear
interpolation type (ref. scipy)


Returns
typing.Tuple

(spectrum, wavenumbers) as np.ndarray




fname = 'files/32990XS01.0'
spectrum, wn = interp(read_opus(fname))


fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12,4))
ax.set_xlim(np.max(wn), np.min(wn))
ax.set(xlabel='Wavenumber', ylabel='Absorbance')
ax.set_axisbelow(True)
ax.grid(True, which='both')
_ = ax.plot(wn, spectrum)

title: Core
text:
source



 read_opus (fname:str)

OPUS (Bruker Optics) infrared spectra reader
It uses patched brukeropusreader third party Python package.
To install it run: conda install -c "spectrocat/label/dev" brukeropusreader









Type
Details




fname
str
file name


Returns
typing.Tuple
(spectrum, wavenumbers) as np.ndarray




fname = 'files/32990XS01.0'
spectrum, wn = read_opus(fname)
print('spectrum:', spectrum)
print('wavenumbers: ', wn)

spectrum: [0.11326975 0.11811147 0.12199899 ... 1.64596999 1.63877058 1.63174832]
wavenumbers:  [7498.04282256 7496.11431361 7494.18580465 ...  603.62330336  601.6947944
  599.76628545]

title: Core
text:
Various utilities

source

select_rows

 select_rows (df:pandas.core.frame.DataFrame, where:Dict[str,Callable])

When passed to Pandas pipe, perform row selection given multiple predicates









Type
Details




df
DataFrame
DataFrame whose rows should be selected


where
typing.Dict[str, typing.Callable]
Pandas columns as keys & predicates as values.


Returns
DataFrame




For instance, given the pd.DataFrame below:

df  = pd.DataFrame({'col1': [1,2,3,4], 'col2': [11, 12, 13, 14]}); df





  
    
      
      col1
      col2
    
  
  
    
      0
      1
      11
    
    
      1
      2
      12
    
    
      2
      3
      13
    
    
      3
      4
      14
    
  




Allow rows selection based on multiple criteria:

df.pipe(select_rows, 
        {'col1': lambda d: d > 1, 'col2': lambda d: not(d % 2)})





  
    
      
      col1
      col2
    
  
  
    
      1
      2
      12
    
    
      3
      4
      14

title: KSSL
text:
The KSSL MIRS/NIRS database is only available on request to USDA/KSSL for now. The following utilities assume you have the database locally (both the Microsoft Access database and Bruker Optics MIRS and NIRS scans).

source

KSSL

 KSSL (src_dir:str)

Initialize self. See help(type(self)) for accurate signature.




Type
Details




src_dir
str
KSSL directory




source


KSSL.to_csv

 KSSL.to_csv (dest_dir:str)

Convert KSSL Microsoft Access DB tables to individual .csv files




Type
Details




dest_dir
str
Destination directory




source


KSSL.get_sample

 KSSL.get_sample ()

Return KSSL DB sample table as pd.DataFrame

Only smp_id > 1000 are selected


source


KSSL.get_layer

 KSSL.get_layer ()

Return KSSL DB layer table as pd.DataFrame

source


KSSL.get_layer_analyte

 KSSL.get_layer_analyte ()

Return KSSL DB layer_analyte table as pd.DataFrame

Only master_prep_id relevant to MIRS analysis selected: [18, 19, 27, 28]
calc_value are by default str as possibly containing values such as (slight, 1:2, …). Only numeric ones are selected.


source


KSSL.get_mirs_mas

 KSSL.get_mirs_mas ()

Return KSSL DB mir_scan_mas_data.csv table as pd.DataFrame

source


KSSL.get_mirs_det

 KSSL.get_mirs_det ()

Return KSSL DB sample table as pd.DataFrame

Only scan_path_name containing valid substring `[‘XN’, ‘XS’]


source

KSSL.bundle

 KSSL.bundle (src_dir_spectra:str, dest_dir:str, analytes:list[int]=[725,
              723, 334], compressed=True)

Bundle KSSL dataset as follows:

/dest_dir/
     /layer_id/
               target.csv





Type
Default
Details




src_dir_spectra
str

Directory containing infrared spectra


dest_dir
str

Destination directory


analytes
list
[725, 723, 334]
Analytes of interest


compressed
bool
True
True if folder should be compressed




kssl = KSSL('../_data')
df = kssl.bundle(dest_dir='../_data/kssl-mirs', 
                 src_dir_spectra='/Volumes/BACKUP/KSSL/MIR_Spectral_Library',
                 analytes=[725, 723, 334])

100%|██████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 177108/177108 [1:04:55<00:00, 45.46it/s]
